title: Tender Chocolate Mexican Chocolate Tostada
description: A fresh and crumbly Mexican-Oaxacan dish featuring rich chocolate and crunchy corn tortillas.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 F (190 C).
In a saucepan, combine the chocolate chips, heavy cream, sugar, cinnamon, and salt.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the chocolate has melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the unsalted butter until it has melted.
Arrange the corn tortillas on a baking sheet and bake for 5 minutes.
Remove the tortillas from the oven and let them cool for 1 minute.
Dip each tortilla into the chocolate mixture, coating both sides evenly.
Place the coated tortillas on a wire rack and let them cool and set for 10 minutes.
Just before serving, top each tostada with a sprinkle of powdered sugar and a fresh mint leaf.
